````
1. Now let me create a comprehensive, production-ready Claude Code prompt for building the use-case PDF generator.
2. 
3. ---
4. 
5. # **CLAUDE CODE PROMPT: SGC TECH AI USE-CASE PDF GENERATOR BUILDER**
6. 
7. ```
8. =============================================================
9. PROJECT BRIEF: SGC TECH AI CUSTOM USE-CASE PDF QUOTE GENERATOR
10. Build Date: April 23, 2026
11. Status: Production-Ready Build (Full-Stack)
12. =============================================================
13. 
14. PROJECT OVERVIEW:
15. ================
16. Build an interactive, web-based quote generator that allows 
17. customers to:
18. 1. Select an industry vertical (Real Estate, Finance, Healthcare, etc.)
19. 2. Choose AI implementation features (optional left column)
20. 3. Choose Odoo ERP modules & features (right column)
21. 4. Combine selections to create a custom package
22. 5. See real-time pricing updates
23. 6. Download a professional PDF proposal with:
24.    - Implementation timeline
25.    - Feature breakdown (AI + ERP)
26.    - Transparent pricing
27.    - ROI calculations
28.    - Next steps & booking CTA
29. 
30. This tool replaces static pricing pages and enables:
31. - Customers to self-serve and create custom packages
32. - SGC to capture high-intent leads
33. - Transparent, non-negotiable pricing (company brand standard)
34. - Automatic PDF generation for follow-up sales
35. 
36. TARGET USERS:
37. =============
38. 1. Website visitors (sgctech.ai) exploring custom solutions
39. 2. SME CFOs / COOs wanting transparent cost breakdown
40. 3. Internal sales team using as demo/pitch tool
41. 4. Consultants and resellers building proposals for clients
42. 
43. BUSINESS REQUIREMENTS:
44. ======================
45. 
46. PRICING MODEL:
47. ==============
48. 
49. BASE PRICING (2026):
50.   1. Industry Selection → Base Cost (setup cost)
51.   2. AI Implementation → Modular pricing per feature
52.   3. Odoo ERP → Per module + integration cost
53.   4. Timeline & Complexity → Adjustment factor
54.   5. Total = Base + (AI modules) + (ERP modules) + Timeline
55. 
56. INDUSTRY VERTICALS & BASE PRICING:
57. ===================================
58. Each industry has:
59. - Base implementation cost (fixed)
60. - Recommended modules (pre-selected)
61. - Average deployment time
62. - Success stories / proof points
63. 
64. 01. REAL ESTATE
65.     Base Cost               : AED 12,000
66.     Avg Deployment Time     : 21 days
67.     Max Concurrent Users    : 100
68.     Proof Point             : OSUS (AED 1.15B volume, 70 agents)
69.     Recommended AI Features : Property valuation, tenant screening,
70.                               commission tracking, deal intelligence
71. 
72. 02. FINANCE & BANKING
73.     Base Cost               : AED 15,000
74.     Avg Deployment Time     : 28 days
75.     Max Concurrent Users    : 200
76.     Proof Point             : Vertex Financial (risk analysis, 
77.                               compliance)
78.     Recommended AI Features : Fraud detection, compliance reporting,
79.                               AML/PEP screening, invoice reconciliation
80. 
81. 03. HEALTHCARE
82.     Base Cost               : AED 14,000
83.     Avg Deployment Time     : 30 days
84.     Max Concurrent Users    : 150
85.     Proof Point             : Hospital operations (AED 1,200 avg 
86.                               patient total)
87.     Recommended AI Features : Triage automation, patient support,
88.                               billing reconciliation, appointment 
89.                               scheduling
90. 
91. 04. RETAIL & ECOMMERCE
92.     Base Cost               : AED 10,000
93.     Avg Deployment Time     : 16 days
94.     Max Concurrent Users    : 500
95.     Proof Point             : Northwind Retail (63% ticket reduction)
96.     Recommended AI Features : Smart search, personalized recommendations,
97.                               support chatbot, inventory forecasting
98. 
99. 05. MANUFACTURING
100.     Base Cost               : AED 13,000
````

1. // Animated circuit-board background — uses <defs> gradients and stroke-dashoffset
2. // for a subtle "current flowing" feel without JS.
3. 
4. export const CircuitBg = () => (
5.   <svg class="circuit-bg" viewBox="0 0 1440 800" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" aria-hidden="true">
6.     <defs>
7.       <linearGradient id="cGrad" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="1">
8.         <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#00d9ff" stop-opacity="0.6" />
9.         <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#0047ff" stop-opacity="0.2" />
10.       </linearGradient>
11.       <filter id="cGlow">
12.         <feGaussianBlur stdDeviation="1.5" result="b" />
13.         <feMerge><feMergeNode in="b" /><feMergeNode in="SourceGraphic" /></feMerge>
14.       </filter>
15.     </defs>
16. 
17.     <g stroke="url(#cGrad)" stroke-width="1" fill="none" filter="url(#cGlow)">
18.       {/* horizontal lanes */}
19.       <path d="M0 120 L200 120 L240 160 L600 160 L640 200 L900 200 L940 160 L1440 160" />
20.       <path d="M0 280 L180 280 L220 240 L520 240 L560 280 L820 280 L860 320 L1440 320" />
21.       <path d="M0 440 L120 440 L160 400 L440 400 L480 440 L760 440 L800 480 L1440 480" />
22.       <path d="M0 600 L220 600 L260 560 L580 560 L620 600 L920 600 L960 640 L1440 640" />
23. 
24.       {/* vertical traces */}
25.       <path d="M300 0 L300 80 L340 120 L340 320 L300 360 L300 800" />
26.       <path d="M720 0 L720 60 L760 100 L760 420 L720 460 L720 800" />
27.       <path d="M1140 0 L1140 100 L1100 140 L1100 380 L1140 420 L1140 800" />
28. 
29.       {/* diagonal feeders */}
30.       <path d="M0 720 L80 640 L280 640 L340 700" />
31.       <path d="M1360 80 L1280 160 L1080 160" />
32.     </g>
33. 
34.     {/* circuit nodes */}
35.     <g fill="#00d9ff">
36.       <circle cx="200" cy="120" r="3" />
37.       <circle cx="600" cy="160" r="3" />
38.       <circle cx="900" cy="200" r="3" />
39.       <circle cx="180" cy="280" r="3" />
40.       <circle cx="520" cy="240" r="3" />
41.       <circle cx="820" cy="280" r="3" />
42.       <circle cx="440" cy="400" r="3" />
43.       <circle cx="760" cy="440" r="3" />
44.       <circle cx="300" cy="360" r="3" />
45.       <circle cx="720" cy="460" r="3" />
46.       <circle cx="1100" cy="380" r="3" />
47.     </g>
48. 
49.     {/* CSS-driven pulses to avoid SMIL animations */}
50.     <g class="circuit-pulses">
51.       <circle class="circuit-pulse one" cx="640" cy="200" r="3" fill="#00d9ff" />
52.       <circle class="circuit-pulse two" cx="760" cy="440" r="3" fill="#00d9ff" />
53.       <circle class="circuit-pulse three" cx="720" cy="460" r="2.5" fill="#00d9ff" />

55.   </svg>

262.         'Deploy with the binding available in this environment',
263.       ],
264.     }, 503)
265.   }
266. 
267.   const key = `aira:session:${sessionId}`
268.   const data = await kv.get(key, { type: 'json' })
269.   return c.json({ ok: true, data: data ?? null })
270. })
271. 
272. app.post('/api/aira/memory', async (c) => {
273.   let body: any = null
274.   try {
275.     body = await c.req.json()
276.   } catch {
277.     return c.json({ ok: false, error: 'Invalid JSON body' }, 400)
278.   }
279. 
280.   const sessionId = typeof body?.sessionId === 'string' ? body.sessionId.trim() : ''
281.   if (!sessionId || sessionId.length > 128) {
282.     return c.json({ ok: false, error: 'sessionId must be a non-empty string up to 128 chars' }, 400)
283.   }
284. 
285.   const kv = (c.env as any)?.AIRA_BRAIN_KV
286.   if (!kv || typeof kv.put !== 'function') {

309.   }
310. 
311.   await kv.put(key, JSON.stringify(payload), {
312.     expirationTtl: 60 * 60 * 24 * 90,
313.     metadata: {

316.       turns: (payload as any)?.brainState?.turns ?? 0,

318.     },
319.   })
320. 
321.   return c.json({ ok: true, saved: true, updatedAt: now })
322. })

```
1. // Two alternating badge designs for SGC TECH AI hero trust row.
2. //
3. // SVG coordinate note:
4. //   viewBox = 192×192, CSS rendered size = 96px → scale = 0.5
5. //   To render text at X px: set SVG font-size = X × 2 (= X / 0.5)
6. //
7. // Variant 1 — Stamp  : serrated circular border, curved arc text
8. // Variant 2 — Award  : clean circle with tick marks, centered icon + text
9. 
10. // ─── Shared SVG math ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
11. 
12. function serratedPath(teeth = 40, cx = 96, cy = 96, outerR = 95, innerR = 86) {
13.   let d = ''
14.   for (let i = 0; i < teeth; i++) {
15.     const a = (i / teeth) * 2 * Math.PI
16.     const r = i % 2 === 0 ? outerR : innerR
17.     const x = (Math.cos(a) * r + cx).toFixed(2)
18.     const y = (Math.sin(a) * r + cy).toFixed(2)
19.     d += i === 0 ? `M ${x} ${y}` : ` L ${x} ${y}`
20.   }
21.   return d + ' Z'
22. }
23. 
24. // Top arc: left→right over the top (sweep=1, clockwise)
25. function topArc(r: number, cx = 96, cy = 96) {
26.   return `M ${cx - r} ${cy} A ${r} ${r} 0 0 1 ${cx + r} ${cy}`
27. }
28. 
29. // Bottom arc: left→right under the bottom (sweep=0, counter-clockwise)
30. function bottomArc(r: number, cx = 96, cy = 96) {
31.   return `M ${cx - r} ${cy} A ${r} ${r} 0 0 0 ${cx + r} ${cy}`
32. }
33. 
34. // Tick marks evenly spaced around a circle
35. function tickMarks(n: number, r: number, len: number, cx = 96, cy = 96) {
36.   let d = ''
37.   for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
38.     const a = (i / n) * 2 * Math.PI
39.     const x1 = (Math.cos(a) * r + cx).toFixed(2)
40.     const y1 = (Math.sin(a) * r + cy).toFixed(2)
41.     const x2 = (Math.cos(a) * (r - len) + cx).toFixed(2)
42.     const y2 = (Math.sin(a) * (r - len) + cy).toFixed(2)
43.     d += `M ${x1} ${y1} L ${x2} ${y2} `
44.   }
45.   return d
46. }
47. 
48. // ─── Badge data ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
49.

59. }

125. const StampBadge = ({ id, title, sub, color, icon, iconViewBox = '0 0 24 24' }: BadgeData) => {
126.   const tId = `t-${id}`, bId = `b-${id}`
127.   return (
128.     <div class="award-badge stamp-variant" style={`--bc:${color}`} title={`${title} — ${sub}`}>
129.       <svg viewBox="0 0 192 192" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" aria-hidden="true">
130.         <defs>
131.           <path id={tId} d={topArc(64)} fill="none" />
132.           <path id={bId} d={bottomArc(64)} fill="none" />
133.         </defs>
134. 
135.         {/* Serrated outer border */}
136.         <path d={serratedPath()} class="ab-serrated" />
137. 
138.         {/* Inner disc */}
139.         <circle cx="96" cy="96" r="79" class="ab-disc" />
140. 
141.         {/* Dashed inner ring */}
142.         <circle cx="96" cy="96" r="70" class="ab-ring" />
143. 
144.         {/* Title arc — top (font-size 20 = 10px at 96px render) */}
145.         <text font-family="'Satoshi',sans-serif" font-size="20" font-weight="700"
146.           letter-spacing="3" text-transform="uppercase" fill="currentColor" class="ab-color">
147.           <textPath href={`#${tId}`} startOffset="50%" textAnchor="middle">{title}</textPath>
148.         </text>
149. 
150.         {/* Sub arc — bottom (font-size 16 = 8px at 96px render) */}
151.         <text font-family="'JetBrains Mono',monospace" font-size="16" font-weight="500"
152.           letter-spacing="2" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.75" class="ab-color">
153.           <textPath href={`#${bId}`} startOffset="50%" textAnchor="middle">{sub}</textPath>
154.         </text>
155. 
156.         {/* Center icon (48×48 in SVG = 24px rendered) */}
157.         <g transform="translate(72,72)">
158.           <svg width="48" height="48" viewBox={iconViewBox}
159.             fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"
160.             stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"
161.             class="ab-color" innerHTML={icon} />
162.         </g>
163.       </svg>
164.     </div>
165.   )
166. }
167. 
168. // ─── Award variant ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
169. // Clean circle with tick marks, level ribbon, icon + text centered.
170. 
171. const AwardBadgeCard = ({ id, title, sub, color, icon, level = 'gold', iconViewBox = '0 0 24 24' }: BadgeData) => {
172.   const [c1, c2] = levelGrad[level]
173.   const gradId = `lg-${id}`
174.   return (
175.     <div class="award-badge award-variant" style={`--bc:${color}`} title={`${title} — ${sub}`}>
176.       <svg viewBox="0 0 192 192" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" aria-hidden="true">
177.         <defs>
178.           <linearGradient id={gradId} x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="1">
179.             <stop offset="0%" stop-color={c1} />
180.             <stop offset="100%" stop-color={c2} />
181.           </linearGradient>
182.         </defs>
183. 
184.         {/* Outer ring with color glow */}
185.         <circle cx="96" cy="96" r="88" class="ab-disc" />
186.         <circle cx="96" cy="96" r="88" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="3" class="ab-color" opacity="0.5" />
187.         <circle cx="96" cy="96" r="80" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1" class="ab-color" opacity="0.25" />
188. 
189.         {/* Tick marks (16 marks, every 22.5°) */}
190.         <path d={tickMarks(16, 88, 8)} stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" class="ab-color" opacity="0.4" />
191. 
192.         {/* Level ribbon pill at top */}
193.         <rect x="62" y="20" width="68" height="22" rx="11" fill={`url(#${gradId})`} />
194.         <text x="96" y="35.5" font-family="'Satoshi',sans-serif" font-size="13"
195.           font-weight="700" letter-spacing="1.5" text-anchor="middle" fill="#0b0e27">
196.           {(level ?? 'gold').toUpperCase()}
197.         </text>
198. 
199.         {/* Center icon */}
200.         <g transform="translate(72,54)">
201.           <svg width="48" height="48" viewBox={iconViewBox}
202.             fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"
203.             stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"
204.             class="ab-color" innerHTML={icon} />
205.         </g>
206. 
207.         {/* Title (font-size 22 = 11px rendered) */}
208.         <text x="96" y="122" font-family="'Satoshi',sans-serif" font-size="22"
209.           font-weight="700" letter-spacing="1" text-anchor="middle"
210.           fill="white">{title}</text>
211. 
212.         {/* Divider line */}
213.         <line x1="60" y1="131" x2="132" y2="131" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1"
214.           class="ab-color" opacity="0.4" />
215. 
216.         {/* Sub text (font-size 17 = 8.5px rendered) */}
217.         <text x="96" y="149" font-family="'JetBrains Mono',monospace" font-size="17"
218.           font-weight="500" letter-spacing="2" text-anchor="middle"
219.           fill="white" opacity="0.7">{sub}</text>
220.       </svg>
221.     </div>
222.   )
223. }
224. 
225. // ─── Row export ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
226. 
227. export const AwardStamps = () => (
228.   <div class="award-stamps reveal reveal-delay-4">
229.     <span class="award-stamps-label">Awards &amp; Compliance</span>
230.     <div class="award-stamps-row">
231.       {badges.map((b) =>
232.         b.variant === 'stamp'
233.           ? <StampBadge {...b} />
234.           : <AwardBadgeCard {...b} />
235.       )}
236.     </div>
237.   </div>
238. )
239.
```

```
1. # SGC TECH AI — Premium Landing Page
2. 
3. ## Project Overview
4. - **Name**: SGC TECH AI Landing Page
5. - **Goal**: A premium, production-ready landing page for SGC TECH AI that reflects world-class design quality — glassmorphism + brutalist minimalism, electric cyan on dark navy, zero generic "AI-slop" aesthetic.
6. - **Features**:
7.   - Asymmetric hero with animated circuit-board SVG background and floating logo with orbit rings
8.   - 8 glassmorphism industry cards with hover spotlight + icon animations
9.   - 6 value-prop cards with neon icon rings and gradient borders
10.   - 3 pricing tiers (Starter $2,400 / Professional $7,900 / Enterprise Custom) with featured card elevation
11.   - 3 customer testimonials with premium card layout and 5-star ratings
12.   - Minimalist FAQ accordion (native `<details>` for zero-JS accessibility)
13.   - High-contrast final CTA card with magnetic buttons
14.   - Trust-bar infinite marquee
15.   - Custom cursor glow (desktop), scroll-reveal observer, magnetic CTAs, animated stat counters
16. 
17. ## URLs
18. - **Sandbox**: https://3000-irbjmooyeepkdfo4opz7l-ea026bf9.sandbox.novita.ai
19. - **Production**: _Not yet deployed to Cloudflare Pages (run `npm run deploy` when ready)_
20. - **Local dev**: http://localhost:3000
21. 
22. ## Entry Points / Sections (anchor links)
23. - `/#top` — Hero
24. - `/#industries` — Industries Served (8 categories)
25. - `/#why` — Why SGC TECH AI (6 value props)
26. - `/#pricing` — Pricing tiers
27. - `/#testimonials` — Customer quotes
28. - `/#faq` — Frequently asked questions
29. - `/#contact` — Final CTA
30. 
31. ## Design System
32. | Token | Value |
33. |---|---|
34. | **Base background** | `#0b0e27` (navy-900) with radial gradient mesh + noise |
35. | **Primary accent** | `#00d9ff` (electric cyan) |
36. | **Secondary accent** | `#0047ff` (tech blue) |
37. | **Display font** | Space Grotesk (600/700) |
38. | **Body font** | Outfit (400/500) |
39. | **Mono font** | JetBrains Mono (labels, numerics) |
40. | **Glass blur** | `backdrop-filter: blur(20px) saturate(160%)` |
41. | **Border system** | Animated gradient border via mask-composite |
42. 
43. ## Anti-Pattern Compliance ✅
44. - ❌ No Inter font — using Space Grotesk + Outfit
45. - ❌ No purple gradients — only cyan/tech-blue
46. - ❌ No centered hero — asymmetric 1.3fr / 1fr grid
47. - ❌ No generic AI-slop aesthetic — intentional brutalist type, mono labels, numbered sections
48. - ❌ No excessive animations — subtle float, pulse, marquee, scroll-reveal only
49. - ❌ No boring solid backgrounds — gradient mesh + noise + animated circuit SVG
50. 
51. ## Data Architecture
52. - **Data Models**: All content is statically typed in `src/index.tsx` (industries, values, pricing, testimonials, faqs arrays)
53. - **Storage Services**: None — 100% static rendered at request time via Hono JSX renderer on Cloudflare Pages edge
54. - **Data Flow**: Request → Hono handler → JSX SSR → Cloudflare edge CDN → Browser
55. 
56. ## User Guide
57. 1. **Navigate** with the top nav (Industries / Why SGC / Pricing / Customers / FAQ) — smooth scroll with offset
58. 2. **Hover** on any glass card to see the cyan spotlight follow the cursor
59. 3. **Hover** on primary CTAs to feel the magnetic pull effect
60. 4. **Expand** FAQ items by clicking (uses native `<details>` for zero-JS accessibility)
```

<sub>⏱️ 5m 55s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

*Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me summarize what I know:

1. **Stack**: Hono.js + JSX for SSR, deployed on Cloudflare Pages, styled with plain CSS (no CSS framework/Tailwind in actual use despite it being in devDependencies), vanilla JS for interactions
2. **Architecture**: Single-file app (`src/index.tsx`) with Hono routes, JSX renderer for SSR, static CSS/JS in `/public/static/`
3. **Build**: `npm run dev` (Vite + @hono/vite-dev-server), `npm run build` (Vite build), `npm run deploy` (build + wrangler pages deploy)


4. **No test framework** is configured, and the design system uses glassmorphism with brutalist minimalism—electric cyan on deep navy with Satoshi and JetBrains Mono fonts. All page content like industries and testimonials lives as typed TypeScript arrays in the main file, and since it's using Hono JSX rather than React, I need to remember the syntax differences like `class` instead of `className` and `innerHTML` for SVG content, with styling handled through static CSS files and vanilla JavaScript.

The app uses a Cloudflare KV binding for memory storage, protects admin routes with an optional token, and drives animations through CSS classes with IntersectionObserver and vanilla JS libraries loaded from CDNs rather than relying on the bundled motion libraries. SVG elements are computed dynamically with math instead of being static, and fonts come from Fontshare and Google Fonts.

Let me check for any `.claude` configuration files or other AI-related setup.*

### 💬 Copilot

Created `.github/copilot-instructions.md`. Here's a summary of what's covered:

- **Commands** — dev, build, deploy, preview (no tests or linting exist)
- **Architecture** — Hono SSR on Cloudflare Pages, the single-file data/routing structure, static asset pipeline, KV binding for Aira memory
- **Key conventions**:
  - Hono JSX vs React (`class` not `className`, `innerHTML` for SVG injection)
  - Static assets (`style.css`, `app.js`) are not bundled — changes apply immediately
  - All page content lives in typed arrays at the top of `src/index.tsx`
  - CSS custom properties as the design token source, glassmorphism/brutalist aesthetic rules
  - The `.reveal` scroll-animation pattern
  - Admin API auth pattern + Cloudflare binding null-check pattern

Would you like to adjust anything or add coverage for areas I may have missed — such as the Aira voice agent data schema, the SVG badge math conventions, or anything about the `copilot.md` planning doc?

588.     opportunities)

603. =============================================================
604. 
605. DATABASE: PostgreSQL
606. 
607. TABLE: industries
608.   id (PK)
609.   name (varchar): "Real Estate", "Finance", etc.
610.   base_cost (integer): AED 12000 (in fils, divide by 100)
611.   avg_deployment_days (integer): 21
612.   max_concurrent_users (integer): 100

614.   proof_point_description (text)

616.   recommended_modules (json): ["crm", "accounting", ...]
617.   icon (varchar): "building-2" (lucide icon name)
618.   created_at (timestamp)
619. 
620. TABLE: ai_features
621.   id (PK)
622.   name (varchar): "AI Chatbot / Virtual Assistant"
623.   description (text): "customer support, onboarding..."
624.   cost (integer): 300000 (AED 3,000 in fils)
625.   tier (enum): "basic" | "advanced" | "specialized"
626.   category (varchar): "chatbot" | "automation" | "analytics"
627.   use_cases (json): ["customer-support", "onboarding"]
628.   monthly_limit (integer): 10000 (queries/month)
629.   max_languages (integer): 2
630.   integrations (json): ["crm", "slack", "telegram"]
631.   created_at (timestamp)
632. 
633. TABLE: odoo_modules
634.   id (PK)
635.   name (varchar): "CRM & Sales Management"
636.   description (text): "Lead & opportunity tracking..."
637.   cost (integer): 350000 (AED 3,500)
638.   category (enum): "core" | "advanced" | "integration"
639.   icon (varchar): "briefcase-2"
640.   features (json): ["lead-tracking", "sales-pipeline", ...]
641.   included_training_hours (integer): 8
642.   included_support_days (integer): 90
643.   integrations (json): ["email", "calendar", "linkedin"]
644.   created_at (timestamp)
645. 
646. TABLE: quotes
647.   id (PK, UUID)
648.   quote_number (varchar): "SGC-2026-001234" (auto-generated)
649.   customer_name (varchar): nullable
650.   customer_email (varchar): nullable

<sub>⏱️ 8m 26s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

*I now have a comprehensive understanding of the full copilot.md brief. Let me analyze what's being asked:

**The Request**: Build a "Custom Use-Case PDF Quote Generator" for SGC TECH AI. This is a full-stack web application that:
1. Lets users select an industry, AI features, and Odoo ERP modules
2. Shows real-time pricing
3. Generates a professional PDF proposal
4. Can email the PDF to customers
5. Stores quote history in a database

**Key technical decisions from copilot.md**:


The stack calls for Next.js 15+ with React 19 on the frontend, PostgreSQL with Prisma on the backend, client-side PDF generation using jsPDF and html2canvas, SendGrid for email delivery, and deployment on Vercel. The design should use a new color palette of #1F2937, #3B82F6, and #10B981 with shadcn/ui and Tailwind CSS for styling.

There's a notable tension here—the current codebase runs on Hono.js with Cloudflare Pages, but the brief specifies Next.js, which represents a fundamental shift in the tech stack and hosting approach.

I need to clarify several architectural decisions before moving forward: whether to build this as a standalone Next.js application deployed separately, integrate it into the existing Hono setup, or add it as a new route within the current Cloudflare Pages infrastructure. The framework choice matters significantly since Next.js is optimized for Vercel while Cloudflare Pages would require an adapter. I also need to confirm whether this should be a separate repository or added to the existing landing page codebase, and whether to stick with the specified jsPDF approach for PDFs or explore server-side alternatives like Puppeteer. Finally, there's the question of the database—whether to use Supabase for PostgreSQL or leverage Cloudflare D1 to stay within the existing infrastructure. The design system question matters too—whether to match the spec's blue palette or align with the existing cyan and navy theme. But really, the stack decision is foundational since it'll shape everything from deployment to styling conventions, so I should lead with that.*

I've read the full `copilot.md`. It's a detailed spec for a **Custom Use-Case PDF Quote Generator** — a multi-step web form where visitors select an industry + AI features + Odoo ERP modules, get real-time pricing, and download/email a professional PDF proposal.

Before I write the plan, I need to resolve a key architectural tension: the spec calls for **Next.js on Vercel**, but this repo is **Hono on Cloudflare Pages**. This choice drives the entire plan.

1. # **YES, ABSOLUTELY! That's Perfect.**
2. 
3. Adding it as a **new page on sgctech.ai** (inside your existing Hono repo) is the **best approach** for your use case.
4. 
5. Here's why this actually works better than I initially suggested:
6. 
7. ---
8. 
9. ## **Why New Page on sgctech.ai is Optimal:**
10. 
11. ### **1. User Experience**
12. - ✅ Users already on `sgctech.ai` → no friction (same domain)
13. - ✅ Seamless flow: Homepage → Pricing → Quote Builder → Book Demo
14. - ✅ Keeps everything in one ecosystem (brand consistent)
15. - ✅ No confused users asking "Is this another company?"
16. 
17. ### **2. SEO & Traffic**
18. - ✅ Quote builder page inherits sgctech.ai's domain authority
19. - ✅ Links from homepage/pricing to `/quote-builder` boost SEO
20. - ✅ Organic traffic flows naturally through the funnel
21. - ✅ Better conversion tracking (all in Google Analytics)
22. 
23. ### **3. Sales Funnel**
24. - ✅ Quote builder is a **lead magnet** on your own domain
25. - ✅ PDF/email captures prospect data naturally
26. - ✅ Can auto-populate CRM with quote data (HubSpot, etc.)
27. - ✅ No external redirects = higher trust, lower bounce
28. 
29. ### **4. Technical Simplicity**
30. - ✅ No separate domain/DNS setup needed
31. - ✅ One Hono + Cloudflare Pages repo (what you already have)
32. - ✅ Shared authentication, styling, branding
33. - ✅ Simpler deployment (push to main, done)
34. 
35. ### **5. Cost**
36. - ✅ Zero extra infrastructure (stays on Cloudflare Pages)
37. - ✅ No extra databases needed (client-side or lightweight Hono DB)
38. - ✅ No Vercel subscription
39. - ✅ Still scales infinitely (Cloudflare handles millions of requests)
40. 
41. ---
42. 
43. ## **Architecture:**
44. 
45. ```
46. sgctech.ai (Hono + Cloudflare Pages)
47. │
48. ├── / (homepage)
49. ├── /pricing
50. ├── /services
51. ├── /about
52. │
53. └── /quote-builder ← NEW PAGE (what we're building)
54.     ├── UI (React client-side)
55.     ├── PDF generation (jsPDF)
56.     ├── Real-time pricing
57.     ├── Email integration (SendGrid)
58.     └── Optional: lightweight DB (D1 - Cloudflare's SQLite)
59. ```
60.
````

</details>

600. FILE: src/utils/pricing.ts
601. 
602. export interface QuoteInput {
603.   industryId: string;
604.   selectedAiFeatures: string[]; // feature IDs
605.   selectedModules: string[]; // module IDs
606.   includeOperationsRetainer: boolean;
607.   implementationDays?: number; // optional override
608. }
609. 
610. export interface QuoteOutput {
611.   baseCost: number;
612.   aiFeaturesCost: number;
613.   modulesCost: number;
614.   implementationSupportCost: number;
615.   operationsRetainerCost: number;
616.   subtotal: number;
617.   vat5Percent: number;
618.   totalCost: number;
619.   estimatedDeploymentDays: number;
620.   estimatedGoLiveDate: string;
621.   lineItems: LineItem[];
622. }
623. 
624. export interface LineItem {
625.   description: string;
626.   quantity: number;
627.   unitPrice: number;
628.   totalAmount: number;
629. }
630. 
631. CALCULATION FORMULA:
632. ====================
633. 
634. const calculateQuote = (input: QuoteInput): QuoteOutput => {
635.   const industry = INDUSTRIES.find(i => i.id === input.industryId);
636.   if (!industry) throw new Error("Industry not found");
637. 
638.   // 1. Base cost
639.   const baseCost = industry.baseCost;
640. 
641.   // 2. AI Features cost (sum selected)
642.   const aiFeaturesCost = input.selectedAiFeatures.reduce((sum, featureId) => {
643.     const feature = AI_FEATURES.find(f => f.id === featureId);
644.     return sum + (feature?.cost || 0);
645.   }, 0);
646. 
647.   // 3. Modules cost (sum selected)
648.   const modulesCost = input.selectedModules.reduce((sum, moduleId) => {
649.     const module = ODOO_MODULES.find(m => m.id === moduleId);
650.     return sum + (module?.cost || 0);
651.   }, 0);
652. 
653.   // 4. Estimate deployment days
654.   let estimatedDeploymentDays = industry.avgDeploymentDays;
655.   const complexityMultiplier = (input.selectedModules.length + 
656.                                 input.selectedAiFeatures.length) / 10;
657.   estimatedDeploymentDays = Math.ceil(
658.     estimatedDeploymentDays * (1 + complexityMultiplier * 0.15)
659.   );
660. 
661.   // 5. Implementation support cost (AED 800/day included)
662.   const implementationSupportCost = estimatedDeploymentDays * 800;
663. 
664.   // 6. Operations retainer (AED 8,000 for 90 days, optional)
665.   const operationsRetainerCost = input.includeOperationsRetainer ? 8000 : 0;
666. 
667.   // 7. Subtotal (before VAT)
668.   const subtotal = baseCost + aiFeaturesCost + modulesCost + 
669.                    implementationSupportCost + operationsRetainerCost;
670. 
671.   // 8. VAT (5% UAE standard)
672.   const vat5Percent = Math.round(subtotal * 0.05);
673. 
674.   // 9. Total
675.   const totalCost = subtotal + vat5Percent;
676. 
677.   // 10. Go-live date (today + deployment days)
678.   const goLiveDate = new Date();
679.   goLiveDate.setDate(goLiveDate.getDate() + estimatedDeploymentDays);
680. 
681.   // 11. Line items (for PDF)
682.   const lineItems: LineItem[] = [
683.     {
684.       description: `Base Implementation (${industry.name})`,
685.       quantity: 1,
686.       unitPrice: baseCost,
687.       totalAmount: baseCost
688.     },
689.     ...input.selectedAiFeatures.map(featureId => {
690.       const feature = AI_FEATURES.find(f => f.id === featureId)!;
691.       return {
692.         description: feature.name,
693.         quantity: 1,
694.         unitPrice: feature.cost,
695.         totalAmount: feature.cost
696.       };
697.     }),
698.     ...input.selectedModules.map(moduleId => {
699.       const module = ODOO_MODULES.find(m => m.id === moduleId)!;
700.       return {
701.         description: module.name,
702.         quantity: 1,
703.         unitPrice: module.cost,
704.         totalAmount: module.cost
705.       };
706.     }),
707.     {
708.       description: `Implementation Support (${estimatedDeploymentDays} days)`,
709.       quantity: estimatedDeploymentDays,
710.       unitPrice: 800,
711.       totalAmount: implementationSupportCost
712.     }
713.   ];
714. 
715.   if (input.includeOperationsRetainer) {
716.     lineItems.push({
717.       description: "90-Day Operations Retainer",
718.       quantity: 1,
719.       unitPrice: 8000,
720.       totalAmount: 8000
721.     });
722.   }
723. 
724.   return {
725.     baseCost,
726.     aiFeaturesCost,
727.     modulesCost,
728.     implementationSupportCost,
729.     operationsRetainerCost,
730.     subtotal,
731.     vat5Percent,
732.     totalCost,
733.     estimatedDeploymentDays,
734.     estimatedGoLiveDate: goLiveDate.toISOString().split('T')[0],
735.     lineItems
736.   };
737. };
738. 
739. export default calculateQuote;
740.

752. export const generateQuotePDF = async (
753.   quoteData: QuoteOutput,
754.   customerName: string,
755.   customerEmail: string,
756.   quoteNumber: string,
757.   industry: Industry
758. ) => {
759.   // Create PDF template as HTML
760.   const htmlContent = `
761.     <!DOCTYPE html>
762.     <html>
763.       <head>
764.         <style>
765.           body { font-family: Arial, sans-serif; color: #1F2937; }
766.           .cover { page-break-after: always; text-align: center; 
767.                    padding: 40px; }
768.           .logo { font-size: 32px; font-weight: bold; color: #3B82F6; 
769.                   margin-bottom: 40px; }
770.           .title { font-size: 24px; font-weight: bold; margin: 20px 0; }
771.           .subtitle { font-size: 14px; color: #6B7280; }
772.           .industry-badge { background: #E0E7FF; padding: 10px 20px; 
773.                             border-radius: 8px; display: inline-block; }
774.           .section { margin: 30px 0; }
775.           .section-title { font-size: 18px; font-weight: bold; 
776.                           color: #1F2937; border-bottom: 2px solid #3B82F6; 
777.                           padding-bottom: 10px; }
778.           table { width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; margin: 20px 0; }
779.           th, td { padding: 12px; text-align: left; border-bottom: 1px solid #E5E7EB; }
780.           th { background: #F3F4F6; font-weight: bold; }
781.           .price-highlight { font-weight: bold; color: #10B981; 
782.                             font-size: 16px; }
783.           .total-row { background: #F3F4F6; font-weight: bold; }
784.           .footer { margin-top: 40px; font-size: 12px; color: #6B7280; }
785.         </style>
786.       </head>
787.       <body>
788.         <!-- COVER PAGE -->
789.         <div class="cover">
790.           <div class="logo">SGC Tech AI</div>
791.           <h1 class="title">Custom Implementation Proposal</h1>
792.           <div class="industry-badge">${industry.name}</div>
793.           <p class="subtitle" style="margin-top: 30px;">Quote #${quoteNumber}</p>
794.           <p class="subtitle">Valid until ${new Date(Date.now() + 30*24*60*60*1000).toLocaleDateString()}</p>
795.           ${customerName ? `<p style="margin-top: 40px; font-size: 16px;">${customerName}</p>` : ''}
796.         </div>
797. 
798.         <!-- EXECUTIVE SUMMARY -->
799.         <div class="section">
800.           <h2 class="section-title">Executive Summary</h2>
801.           <p>You are implementing a custom solution combining:</p>
802.           <ul>
803.             <li>${quoteData.lineItems.filter(l => !l.description.includes('Support') && !l.description.includes('Retainer')).length} modules and AI features</li>
804.             <li>Deployment in ${quoteData.estimatedDeploymentDays} days (go-live: ${quoteData.estimatedGoLiveDate})</li>
805.             <li>Transparent, fixed pricing with no surprises</li>
806.             <li>24/7 support included in first 90 days</li>
807.           </ul>
808.         </div>
809. 
810.         <!-- IMPLEMENTATION BREAKDOWN -->
811.         <div class="section">
812.           <h2 class="section-title">Implementation Breakdown</h2>
813.           <table>
814.             <thead>
815.               <tr>
816.                 <th>Feature / Module</th>
817.                 <th>Category</th>
818.                 <th>Price</th>
819.               </tr>
820.             </thead>
821.             <tbody>
822.               ${quoteData.lineItems.map(item => `
823.                 <tr>
824.                   <td>${item.description}</td>
825.                   <td>${item.quantity > 1 ? `${item.quantity}×` : ''}</td>
826.                   <td class="price-highlight">AED ${item.totalAmount.toLocaleString()}</td>
827.                 </tr>
828.               `).join('')}
829.             </tbody>
830.           </table>
831.         </div>
832. 
833.         <!-- PRICING BREAKDOWN -->
834.         <div class="section">
835.           <h2 class="section-title">Pricing Breakdown</h2>
836.           <table>
837.             <tbody>
838.               <tr>
839.                 <td>Subtotal (before VAT)</td>
840.                 <td class="price-highlight">AED ${quoteData.subtotal.toLocaleString()}</td>
841.               </tr>
842.               <tr>
843.                 <td>VAT (5%)</td>
844.                 <td class="price-highlight">AED ${quoteData.vat5Percent.toLocaleString()}</td>
845.               </tr>
846.               <tr class="total-row">
847.                 <td>TOTAL PRICE</td>
848.                 <td class="price-highlight" style="font-size: 18px;">AED ${quoteData.totalCost.toLocaleString()}</td>
849.               </tr>
850.             </tbody>
851.           </table>
852.         </div>
853.

868.             <li>Sign off and schedule kick-off meeting</li>

877.         <!-- COMPLIANCE -->

881.           <p>UAE VAT applied. All prices in AED.</p>
882.         </div>
883.       </body>
884.     </html>
885.   `;
886.

910. SIMPLIFIED APPROACH (Better):
911. ==============================
912. Instead of html2canvas, use template HTML + jsPDF directly:
913. 
914. import jsPDF from 'jspdf';
915. 
916. export const generateQuotePDF = (
917.   quoteData: QuoteOutput,
918.   customerName: string,
919.   quoteNumber: string,
920.   industry: Industry
921. ) => {
922.   const doc = new jsPDF();
923.   
924.   // Title
925.   doc.setFontSize(20);
926.   doc.text('SGC Tech AI', 20, 20);
927.   doc.setFontSize(16);
928.   doc.text('Custom Implementation Proposal', 20, 35);
929.   
930.   // Industry & metadata
931.   doc.setFontSize(11);
932.   doc.text(`Industry: ${industry.name}`, 20, 50);
933.   doc.text(`Quote #: ${quoteNumber}`, 20, 60);
934.   doc.text(`Customer: ${customerName}`, 20, 70);
935.   
936.   // Pricing table
937.   let yPosition = 90;
938.   doc.setFontSize(12);
939.   doc.text('Pricing Breakdown', 20, yPosition);
940.   yPosition += 10;
941.   
942.   quoteData.lineItems.forEach(item => {
943.     doc.text(`${item.description}`, 20, yPosition);
944.     doc.text(`AED ${item.totalAmount}`, 170, yPosition, { align: 'right' });
945.     yPosition += 8;
946.   });
947.   
948.   // Total
949.   yPosition += 5;
950.   doc.setFontSize(13);
951.   doc.setFont(undefined, 'bold');
952.   doc.text(`TOTAL: AED ${quoteData.totalCost}`, 20, yPosition);
953.   
954.   // Save
955.   doc.save(`SGC-Quote-${quoteNumber}.pdf`);
956. };

964. import React, { useState } from 'react';
965. import { QUOTE_DATA } from '@/data/quote-data';
966. import calculateQuote from '@/utils/pricing';
967. import IndustrySelector from './IndustrySelector';
968. import FeatureSelector from './FeatureSelector';
969. import PricingSummary from './PricingSummary';
970. import TimelineDisplay from './TimelineDisplay';
971. import CustomerInfo from './CustomerInfo';
972. import { generateQuotePDF } from '@/utils/pdf-generator';
973. 
974. export default function QuoteBuilder() {
975.   const [selectedIndustry, setSelectedIndustry] = useState<string>('');
976.   const [selectedAiFeatures, setSelectedAiFeatures] = useState<string[]>([]);
977.   const [selectedModules, setSelectedModules] = useState<string[]>([]);
978.   const [includeRetainer, setIncludeRetainer] = useState(false);
979.   const [customerName, setCustomerName] = useState('');
980.   const [customerEmail, setCustomerEmail] = useState('');
981.   const [showCustomerModal, setShowCustomerModal] = useState(false);
982. 
983.   const industry = QUOTE_DATA.industries.find(i => i.id === selectedIndustry);
984.   
985.   const quote = selectedIndustry ? calculateQuote({
986.     industryId: selectedIndustry,
987.     selectedAiFeatures,
988.     selectedModules,
989.     includeOperationsRetainer: includeRetainer
990.   }) : null;
991. 
992.   const handleDownloadPDF = () => {
993.     if (!customerName.trim()) {
994.       alert('Please enter your name');
995.       return;
996.     }
997.     if (!quote || !industry) return;
998.     
999.     const quoteNumber = `SGC-${Date.now()}`;
1000.     generateQuotePDF(quote, customerName, quoteNumber, industry);
1001.   };
1002. 
1003.   return (
1004.     <div className="min-h-screen bg-gradient-to-br from-gray-50 to-gray-100 py-12 px-4">
1005.       <div className="max-w-7xl mx-auto">
1006.         {/* Header */}
1007.         <div className="text-center mb-12">
1008.           <h1 className="text-4xl font-bold text-gray-900 mb-4">
1009.             Build Your Custom Package
1010.           </h1>
1011.           <p className="text-lg text-gray-600 max-w-2xl mx-auto">
1012.             Select your industry, choose AI features and ERP modules, 
1013.             see transparent pricing, and download your proposal instantly.
1014.           </p>
1015.         </div>
1016. 
1017.         {/* Step 1: Industry Selection */}
1018.         <IndustrySelector
1019.           industries={QUOTE_DATA.industries}
1020.           selected={selectedIndustry}
1021.           onSelect={setSelectedIndustry}
1022.         />
1023. 
1024.         {selectedIndustry && industry && (
1025.           <>
1026.             {/* Step 2: Two-Column Feature Selection */}
1027.             <div className="grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-8 mt-12">
1028.               {/* Left Column: AI Features */}
1029.               <div className="lg:col-span-1">
1030.                 <FeatureSelector
1031.                   title="AI Features (Optional)"
1032.                   items={QUOTE_DATA.aiFeatures}
1033.                   selected={selectedAiFeatures}
1034.                   onSelect={setSelectedAiFeatures}
1035.                 />
1036.               </div>
1037. 
1038.               {/* Middle Column: ERP Modules */}
1039.               <div className="lg:col-span-1">
1040.                 <FeatureSelector
1041.                   title="Odoo ERP Modules"
1042.                   items={QUOTE_DATA.odooModules}
1043.                   selected={selectedModules}
1044.                   onSelect={setSelectedModules}
1045.                 />
1046.               </div>
1047. 
1048.               {/* Right Column: Pricing Summary (Sticky) */}
1049.               <div className="lg:col-span-1">
1050.                 <div className="sticky top-4">
1051.                   {quote && (
1052.                     <>
1053.                       <PricingSummary quote={quote} industry={industry} />
1054.                       <TimelineDisplay quote={quote} />
1055.                       
1056.                       {/* Retainer toggle */}
1057.                       <div className="mt-6 p-4 bg-blue-50 rounded-lg border border-blue-200">
1058.                         <label className="flex items-center gap-3 cursor-pointer">
1059.                           <input
1060.                             type="checkbox"
1061.                             checked={includeRetainer}
1062.                             onChange={(e) => setIncludeRetainer(e.target.checked)}
1063.                             className="w-4 h-4"
1064.                           />
1065.                           <div>
1066.                             <span className="font-semibold text-blue-900">
1067.                               Include 90-Day Operations Retainer?
1068.                             </span>
1069.                             <p className="text-sm text-blue-700">
1070.                               AED 8,000 (24/7 priority support)
1071.                             </p>
1072.                           </div>
1073.                         </label>
1074.                       </div>
1075. 
1076.                       {/* CTA Buttons */}
1077.                       <button
1078.                         onClick={() => setShowCustomerModal(true)}
1079.                         className="w-full mt-6 bg-blue-600 hover:bg-blue-700 text-white font-bold py-3 px-4 rounded-lg transition"
1080.                       >
1081.                         Generate & Download PDF
1082.                       </button>
1083.                     </>
1084.                   )}
1085.                 </div>
1086.               </div>
1087.             </div>
1088. 
1089.             {/* Customer Info Modal */}
1090.             {showCustomerModal && (
1091.               <CustomerInfo
1092.                 onClose={() => setShowCustomerModal(false)}
1093.                 onGenerate={(name, email) => {
1094.                   setCustomerName(name);
1095.                   setCustomerEmail(email);
1096.                   handleDownloadPDF();
1097.                   setShowCustomerModal(false);
1098.                 }}
1099.               />
1100.             )}
1101.           </>
1102.         )}
1103.       </div>
1104.     </div>
1105.   );
1106. }
1107. 
1108. CHILD COMPONENTS:
1109. 
1110. // src/components/QuoteBuilder/IndustrySelector.tsx
1111. export default function IndustrySelector({ industries, selected, onSelect }) {
1112.   return (
1113.     <div className="grid grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-5 gap-4">
1114.       {industries.map(industry => (
1115.         <button
1116.           key={industry.id}
1117.           onClick={() => onSelect(industry.id)}
1118.           className={`p-4 rounded-lg border-2 transition ${
1119.             selected === industry.id
1120.               ? 'border-blue-600 bg-blue-50'
1121.               : 'border-gray-200 hover:border-blue-300'
1122.           }`}
1123.         >
1124.           <div className="text-3xl mb-2">{getIcon(industry.icon)}</div>
1125.           <div className="font-semibold text-sm">{industry.name}</div>
1126.           <div className="text-xs text-gray-600 mt-1">
1127.             AED {industry.baseCost.toLocaleString()}
1128.           </div>
1129.         </button>
1130.       ))}
1131.     </div>
1132.   );
1133. }
1134. 
1135. // src/components/QuoteBuilder/FeatureSelector.tsx
1136. export default function FeatureSelector({ title, items, selected, onSelect }) {
1137.   const [expanded, setExpanded] = useState(true);
1138.   const categories = [...new Set(items.map(i => i.category))];
1139. 
1140.   return (
1141.     <div className="bg-white rounded-lg shadow-md p-6">
1142.       <h3 className="text-lg font-bold text-gray-900 mb-4">{title}</h3>
1143.       {categories.map(category => (
1144.         <div key={category} className="mb-4">
1145.           <h4 className="text-sm font-semibold text-gray-700 mb-2 capitalize">
1146.             {category}
1147.           </h4>
1148.           {items
1149.             .filter(item => item.category === category)
1150.             .map(item => (
1151.               <label key={item.id} className="flex items-start gap-3 mb-3 p-2 hover:bg-gray-50 rounded cursor-pointer">
1152.                 <input
1153.                   type="checkbox"
1154.                   checked={selected.includes(item.id)}
1155.                   onChange={(e) => {
1156.                     if (e.target.checked) {
1157.                       onSelect([...selected, item.id]);
1158.                     } else {
1159.                       onSelect(selected.filter(id => id !== item.id));
1160.                     }
1161.                   }}
1162.                   className="w-4 h-4 mt-1"
1163.                 />
1164.                 <div className="flex-1">
1165.                   <div className="font-semibold text-sm text-gray-900">

<sub>⏱️ 14m 1s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

*Now I have a thorough understanding of the spec. Let me analyze the key architectural concerns and create a solid implementation plan.

**Summary of the spec (updated copilot.md)**:
- Build a `/quote-builder` page as a new route in the **existing Hono + Cloudflare Pages repo**
- Client-side React components for the UI (but the existing Hono app uses Hono JSX, not React)
- All data hardcoded in TypeScript (no database for MVP)
- jsPDF for PDF generation
- The spec mentions React components with `className` (Tailwind) — but the existing Hono app uses Hono JSX with `class` and plain CSS


- The React components rely on `useState` hooks for interactive state, which creates a fundamental incompatibility with the current Hono JSX setup

The core issue is that adding React would require modifying the Vite config to include the React plugin, but the existing build pipeline is specifically configured for Hono JSX. I need to decide whether to retrofit React into the existing setup or stick with Hono JSX and adapt the spec accordingly.

The most pragmatic approach is to add React as a client-side bundle specifically for the quote builder page, keeping the Hono SSR for the rest of the application. This respects both the spec's intent and the existing architecture. The CSS is already organized in `/public/static/style.css`, so I can work within that structure.

To make this work, I'd need to configure Vite to build a separate client-side React entry point alongside the existing server build. The Hono route would serve an HTML shell for `/quote-builder`, and a bundled React component would mount on the client side. However, the current Vite setup with `@hono/vite-build` doesn't handle client-side React bundles yet—I'd need to add `@vitejs/plugin-react` and adjust the configuration. That said, there's a simpler MVP option: stick with server-side Hono JSX for the form itself, which avoids the extra complexity.

For the MVP, I'm leaning toward using Hono JSX with vanilla JavaScript for the interactive parts like price calculations and PDF generation, keeping everything on the existing tech stack. I'll load jsPDF from a CDN to avoid build changes.

On styling, the spec calls for Tailwind but the site uses plain CSS, so I'll match the existing design system and swap in the site's cyan color scheme instead of the spec's blue. The real-time price updates that the spec requires with React hooks can be handled with vanilla JS event listeners instead.

There's a design conflict though — the spec shows a light theme while the site is dark-themed with navy and cyan. I need to decide whether to follow the existing site's visual language or create a separate design for the quote builder. Looking back at the spec's claim that Tailwind is already configured, I'm realizing the site has Tailwind set up in the build pipeline but doesn't actually use utility classes in the markup.

For the MVP, I'm thinking I should stick with the existing Hono JSX and vanilla JavaScript stack, create a new route for the quote builder, and use plain CSS that extends the current dark theme rather than introducing Tailwind or a new design system. I can pull in jsPDF from a CDN for the PDF generation, similar to how typed.js is being used.

But there's also the option of adding React to the build pipeline with `@vitejs/plugin-react` and creating a separate client-side React bundle—that would align more closely with the spec, though it means more work on the build configuration. Since the spec was written for this Hono codebase and specifically mentions React components, and the project already has framer-motion in dependencies, adding React might actually be feasible without too much friction. Looking at the Vite configuration, `@hono/vite-build/cloudflare-pages` compiles the Hono app as an edge function with output in `dist/`, while static files from `public/` are served separately — so I could add a client bundle entry point that gets built alongside the server code and referenced via a script tag in the HTML.

However, mixing server and client builds through the Hono plugin gets complicated, so I'm going with the simpler approach: keep the page structure as Hono JSX for server-side rendering, write the interactive logic as vanilla JavaScript in `public/static/quote-builder.js`, and load jsPDF from a CDN. This fits the existing architecture without needing any build pipeline changes.

The React components from the spec translate naturally into Hono JSX for the layout, vanilla JavaScript for managing selections and state, DOM updates for live price calculations, and jsPDF for generating the PDF. This keeps things lightweight—no React dependency needed, no Tailwind changes, no new npm packages, and it matches exactly how the site is already structured.

Now I'm mapping out the full implementation plan...

Building the data layer with industry definitions, AI features, and Odoo modules, then creating a pricing utility to calculate totals based on selections. The Hono route will serve the quote builder page with the JSX structure, while new CSS in the stylesheet handles the visual design. Client-side JavaScript manages the interactive state, real-time price updates, and PDF generation using jsPDF from a CDN. I'll register the route in the main index file, add a navigation link, and leave room for Phase 2 features like D1 database storage and SendGrid integration.

Adding styles...*

<sub>⏱️ 16m 5s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Plan is saved. Here's the summary:

---

## What the plan covers

**Phase 1 — MVP (6 tasks, fully self-contained):**

| # | Task | Key detail |
|---|------|-----------|
| 1 | `src/data/quote-data.ts` | 10 industries, 9 AI features, 15 Odoo modules — all hardcoded from the spec |
| 2 | `src/utils/pricing.ts` | `calculateQuote()` with complexity-adjusted days, 5% UAE VAT, line items |
| 3 | `src/routes/quote-builder.tsx` | Hono JSX page — industry grid → two-column selector → sticky pricing sidebar → customer modal |
| 4 | `src/index.tsx` | Mount route + add "Quote Builder" nav link + pricing section CTA |
| 5 | `public/static/quote-builder.js` | Vanilla JS state, real-time price updates, jsPDF generation (branded dark navy PDF) |
| 6 | `public/static/style.css` | All `.qb-*` styles using existing design tokens (dark theme, not the light spec) |

**Phase 2 (3 tasks, after MVP):** D1 quote persistence, SendGrid email-PDF, shareable links.

**Key architectural decisions baked into the plan:**
- **No React** — follows the existing Hono JSX + vanilla JS pattern; no build pipeline changes
- **Dark navy theme** — uses site's existing CSS tokens, not the light theme from the spec
- **jsPDF via CDN** — same approach as Typed.js and particles.js
- **No database for Phase 1** — quote number is ephemeral, all state in browser

Want to adjust the scope, design approach, or Phase 2 priorities before we start building?

<details>

```
1. # Plan: /quote-builder Page — SGC TECH AI
2. 
3. ## Problem & Approach
4. 
5. Add an interactive `/quote-builder` page to the existing Hono + Cloudflare Pages site. Customers select an industry, AI features, and Odoo ERP modules, see real-time pricing, then download a branded PDF proposal.
6. 
7. **Key architectural decision**: The spec references React components with `className`/Tailwind, but the existing site uses Hono JSX with `class` and plain CSS, plus vanilla JS via `public/static/app.js`. To avoid touching the Vite/Hono build pipeline (no `@vitejs/plugin-react` needed), we follow the **existing pattern exactly**:
8. - Hono JSX handles the server-rendered page shell (`src/routes/quote-builder.tsx`)
9. - All client-side interactivity (state, price updates, PDF) lives in `public/static/quote-builder.js`
10. - jsPDF loaded via CDN (same as Typed.js, particles.js)
11. - Styles extend the existing design system in `public/static/style.css`
12. 
13. This means no new build dependencies, no pipeline changes, and the page is consistent with the main site's dark navy glassmorphism aesthetic.
14. 
15. ---
16. 
17. ## Todos
18. 
19. ### Phase 1 — MVP (core deliverable)
20.
```

</details>

324. const isAdminAuthorized = (c: any) => {
325.   const requiredToken = (c.env as any)?.ADMIN_TOKEN
326.   if (!requiredToken) return true
327. 
328.   const queryToken = c.req.query('token')
329.   const headerToken = c.req.header('x-admin-token')
330.   return queryToken === requiredToken || headerToken === requiredToken
331. }
332. 
333. app.get('/api/admin/aira/sessions', async (c) => {
334.   if (!isAdminAuthorized(c)) {
335.     return c.json({ ok: false, error: 'Unauthorized' }, 401)
336.   }
337. 
338.   const kv = (c.env as any)?.AIRA_BRAIN_KV
339.   if (!kv || typeof kv.list !== 'function') {
340.     return c.json({
341.       ok: false,
342.       error: 'AIRA_BRAIN_KV binding is missing',
343.     }, 503)
344.   }
345. 
346.   const limit = Math.min(Number(c.req.query('limit') || 20), 50)
347.   const cursor = c.req.query('cursor') || undefined
348.   const includeDetail = c.req.query('detail') === 'true'
349. 
350.   const listed = await kv.list({
351.     prefix: 'aira:session:',
352.     limit,
353.     cursor,
354.   })
355. 
356.   const sessions = await Promise.all(listed.keys.map(async (k: any) => {
357.     const sessionId = String(k.name).replace('aira:session:', '')
358.     const meta = (k as any).metadata || {}
359. 
360.     let detail: any = null
361.     if (includeDetail) {
362.       detail = await kv.get(k.name, { type: 'json' })
363.     }
364. 
365.     if (!includeDetail && !meta.updatedAt) {
366.       const raw = await kv.get(k.name, { type: 'json' })
367.       if (raw) {
368.         return {
369.           sessionId,
370.           updatedAt: (raw as any).updatedAt ?? null,
371.           leadScore: (raw as any)?.brainState?.leadScore ?? 0,
372.           turns: (raw as any)?.brainState?.turns ?? 0,
373.           messageCount: Array.isArray((raw as any)?.history) ? (raw as any).history.length : 0,
374.           summary: {
375.             company: (raw as any)?.brainState?.profile?.company ?? '',
376.             role: (raw as any)?.brainState?.profile?.role ?? '',
377.             lastIntent: (raw as any)?.brainState?.lastIntent ?? '',
378.           },
379.         }
380.       }
```

473.       </section>

530.             } catch (err) {
531.               status.textContent = 'Error loading sessions.'
532.             }
533.           }
534. 
535.           const loadSessionDetail = async (sessionId) => {
536.             status.textContent = 'Loading detail for ' + sessionId + '...'
537.             try {
538.               const q = new URLSearchParams()
539.               if (state.token) q.set('token', state.token)
540.               const res = await fetch('/api/admin/aira/session/' + encodeURIComponent(sessionId) + (q.toString() ? ('?' + q.toString()) : ''))
541.               const payload = await res.json()
542.               if (!res.ok || !payload.ok) {
543.                 status.textContent = payload.error || 'Failed to load detail.'
544.                 return
545.               }
546.               detail.textContent = JSON.stringify(payload.detail || { message: 'No detail found.' }, null, 2)
547.               status.textContent = 'Detail loaded for ' + sessionId + '.'
548.             } catch {
549.               status.textContent = 'Error loading detail.'
550.             }
551.           }
552. 
553.           body.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
554.             const target = e.target
555.             if (!(target instanceof HTMLElement)) return
556.             const sessionId = target.getAttribute('data-session')
557.             if (!sessionId) return
558.             loadSessionDetail(sessionId)
559.           })
560. 
561.           refreshBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
562.             state.cursor = ''
563.             loadSessions(false)
564.           })
565. 
566.           loadMoreBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
567.             if (!state.cursor) return
568.             loadSessions(true)
569.           })
570. 
571.           loadSessions(false)
572.         })()
573.       `}</script>
574.     </main>

<sub>⏱️ 24m 36s</sub>

### 💭 Reasoning

*The renderer uses `{ children }` only, not `{ children, title }`. The `c.render()` just passes the JSX as children. Since jsPDF is loaded globally via renderer, I need to add the quote-builder.js script. But since it's loaded via renderer for all pages, that's a potential overhead on the main page. However, the quote-builder.js only has an IIFE that checks for `document.getElementById('qb-data')`, so it won't cause issues on other pages.

Actually, I should add the `quote-builder.js` script only on the quote-builder page. 

I can include a `\<script\>` tag directly in the JSX of the route itself, and since Hono wraps the rendered JSX in a body tag, it'll execute properly. I'll add the script tag at the bottom of the main element in the quote-builder route.*

## ✅ `/quote-builder` Phase 1 Complete

All 6 Phase 1 todos are done and the build passes cleanly (`npm run build` — 382ms, 0 errors).

### Files created/modified:

| File | Status |
|------|--------|
| `src/data/quote-data.ts` | ✅ Created — 10 industries, 9 AI features, 15 Odoo modules with full AED pricing |
| `src/utils/pricing.ts` | ✅ Created — `calculateQuote()` with complexity-adjusted days + 5% UAE VAT |
| `src/routes/quote-builder.tsx` | ✅ Created — Full Hono JSX SSR page with server-rendered industry/feature/module selectors |
| `public/static/quote-builder.js` | ✅ Created — Vanilla JS IIFE: state management, real-time pricing, 3-page PDF generation |
| `public/static/style.css` | ✅ Extended — All `.qb-*` classes using existing dark navy design tokens |
| `src/index.tsx` | ✅ Modified — Route mounted via `app.route()`, nav link added, pricing CTA added |
| `src/renderer.tsx` | ✅ Modified — jsPDF + autoTable CDN scripts added |

### How it works:
1. Visit `/quote-builder` → SSR page with all 10 industry cards
2. Click an industry → builder reveals, pricing sidebar activates
3. Check AI features / Odoo modules → prices update instantly in the sidebar
4. Toggle "90-Day Operations Retainer" → AED 8,000 added
5. Click "Generate PDF Proposal" → enter name/company → 3-page branded dark navy PDF downloads (cover page, itemized pricing table, timeline + next steps)

### Pricing formula:
```
days = ceil(industry.avgDeploymentDays × (1 + (aiCount + moduleCount) / 10 × 0.15))
subtotal = base + aiFeatures + modules + (days × 800) + (retainer ? 8,000 : 0)
vat = round(subtotal × 0.05)
total = subtotal + vat
```
````

## ✅ `/quote-builder` Phase 1 Complete

All 6 Phase 1 todos are done and the build passes cleanly (`npm run build` — 382ms, 0 errors).

### Files created/modified:

| File | Status |
|------|--------|
| `src/data/quote-data.ts` | ✅ Created — 10 industries, 9 AI features, 15 Odoo modules with full AED pricing |
| `src/utils/pricing.ts` | ✅ Created — `calculateQuote()` with complexity-adjusted days + 5% UAE VAT |
| `src/routes/quote-builder.tsx` | ✅ Created — Full Hono JSX SSR page with server-rendered industry/feature/module selectors |
| `public/static/quote-builder.js` | ✅ Created — Vanilla JS IIFE: state management, real-time pricing, 3-page PDF generation |
| `public/static/style.css` | ✅ Extended — All `.qb-*` classes using existing dark navy design tokens |
| `src/index.tsx` | ✅ Modified — Route mounted via `app.route()`, nav link added, pricing CTA added |

### How it works:
1. Visit `/quote-builder` → SSR page with all 10 industry cards
2. Click an industry → builder reveals, pricing sidebar activates
3. Check AI features / Odoo modules → prices update instantly in the sidebar
4. Toggle "90-Day Operations Retainer" → AED 8,000 added
5. Click "Generate PDF Proposal" → enter name/company → 3-page branded dark navy PDF downloads (cover page, itemized pricing table, timeline + next steps)

### Pricing formula:
```
days = ceil(industry.avgDeploymentDays × (1 + (aiCount + moduleCount) / 10 × 0.15))
subtotal = base + aiFeatures + modules + (days × 800) + (retainer ? 8,000 : 0)
vat = round(subtotal × 0.05)
total = subtotal + vat
```

````
1. # Plan: /quote-builder Page — SGC TECH AI
2. 
3. ## Problem & Approach
4. 
5. Add an interactive `/quote-builder` page to the existing Hono + Cloudflare Pages site. Customers select an industry, AI features, and Odoo ERP modules, see real-time pricing, then download a branded PDF proposal.
6. 
7. **Key architectural decision**: The spec references React components with `className`/Tailwind, but the existing site uses Hono JSX with `class` and plain CSS, plus vanilla JS via `public/static/app.js`. To avoid touching the Vite/Hono build pipeline (no `@vitejs/plugin-react` needed), we follow the **existing pattern exactly**:
8. - Hono JSX handles the server-rendered page shell (`src/routes/quote-builder.tsx`)
9. - All client-side interactivity (state, price updates, PDF) lives in `public/static/quote-builder.js`
10. - jsPDF loaded via CDN (same as Typed.js, particles.js)
11. - Styles extend the existing design system in `public/static/style.css`
12. 
13. This means no new build dependencies, no pipeline changes, and the page is consistent with the main site's dark navy glassmorphism aesthetic.
14. 
15. ---
16. 
17. ## Todos
18. 
19. ### Phase 1 — MVP (core deliverable)
20. 
21. 1. **data-file** — Create `src/data/quote-data.ts`
22.    - Export `INDUSTRIES` (10 verticals with id, name, baseCost, avgDeploymentDays, proofPoint, recommendedAiFeatures, recommendedModules, icon)
23.    - Export `AI_FEATURES` (9 features across Tier 1/2/3 with id, name, description, cost, tier, category, icon)
24.    - Export `ODOO_MODULES` (15 modules across core/advanced/integration with id, name, description, cost, category, features, icon)
25.    - All data as typed TypeScript const arrays (exact values from copilot.md spec)
26. 
27. 2. **pricing-util** — Create `src/utils/pricing.ts`
28.    - `QuoteInput` interface (industryId, selectedAiFeatures[], selectedModules[], includeOperationsRetainer)
29.    - `QuoteOutput` interface (baseCost, aiFeaturesCost, modulesCost, implementationSupportCost, operationsRetainerCost, subtotal, vat5Percent, totalCost, estimatedDeploymentDays, estimatedGoLiveDate, lineItems[])
30.    - `calculateQuote(input)` function implementing the formula from spec:
31.      - `subtotal = base + AI features sum + modules sum + (days × 800) + (retainer ? 8000 : 0)`
32.      - Complexity adjustment: `days = ceil(industryDays × (1 + (features + modules) / 10 × 0.15))`
33.      - `vat = round(subtotal × 0.05)`, `total = subtotal + vat`
34.    - This module is imported by the Hono route for SSR rendering (initial state) and its logic is duplicated in the client JS for real-time updates
35. 
36. 3. **hono-route** — Create `src/routes/quote-builder.tsx`
37.    - Register `GET /quote-builder` handler
38.    - Uses `renderer` from `src/renderer.tsx` (shared head/body shell)
39.    - Renders full JSX page markup server-side with all static content
40.    - Structure: nav (reuse), hero section, Step 1 industry grid, Step 2 two-column (AI features left, ERP modules right), Step 3 sticky pricing sidebar, Step 4 customer modal (hidden by default), footer CTA
41.    - All 10 industries rendered as clickable cards with `data-industry-id` attributes
42.    - All AI features and ERP modules rendered as checkboxes with `data-feature-id` / `data-module-id` attributes and `data-cost` for JS consumption
43.    - Pricing summary section has span elements with IDs for DOM updates (`#qb-base-cost`, `#qb-ai-cost`, etc.)
44.    - Includes `<script src="/static/quote-builder.js" defer></script>` and jsPDF CDN
45. 
46. 4. **route-register** — Update `src/index.tsx`
47.    - Import and mount the quote-builder route: `app.route('/quote-builder', quoteBuilderApp)`
48.    - Add "Quote Builder" link to the navigation bar JSX
49.    - Add a CTA link/button in the pricing section pointing to `/quote-builder`
50. 
51. 5. **client-js** — Create `public/static/quote-builder.js`
52.    - State object: `{ industryId: null, aiFeatures: Set(), modules: Set(), includeRetainer: false, customerName: '', customerEmail: '' }`
53.    - Event delegation on industry cards (click → toggle selected, update state)
54.    - Event delegation on checkboxes (change → toggle feature/module in Set, recalculate)
55.    - `calculateQuote()` function — mirrors the TypeScript pricing util (same formula in JS)
56.    - `updatePricingUI()` — updates all DOM spans with formatted AED amounts
57.    - `showStep2()` — reveals two-column selector after industry chosen
58.    - `showCustomerModal()` / `hideCustomerModal()` — modal open/close
59.    - `generatePDF(customerName)` — builds jsPDF document (see Section 4 of spec: cover page, executive summary, implementation breakdown table, pricing breakdown, timeline, next steps, compliance footer)
60.    - PDF quote number format: `SGC-${Date.now().toString().slice(-6)}`
61.    - `downloadPDF()` → `doc.save('SGC-Quote-XXXXXX.pdf')`
62. 
63. 6. **css-styles** — Add quote-builder styles to `public/static/style.css`
64.    - Extend existing design tokens (dark navy theme, cyan accent)
65.    - `.qb-page` — page container, padding, max-width
66.    - `.qb-industries` — 5-column grid (responsive down to 2)
67.    - `.qb-industry-card` — glass card with hover spotlight (reuse `.glass` patterns), `data-selected` variant with cyan border
68.    - `.qb-columns` — two-column layout (feature cols + sticky sidebar)
69.    - `.qb-feature-col` — scrollable feature list column
70.    - `.qb-checkbox-item` — checkbox row with label, description, price
71.    - `.qb-sidebar` — sticky pricing sidebar
72.    - `.qb-pricing-table` — subtotal rows, total row with cyan highlight
73.    - `.qb-timeline` — step-by-step timeline display
74.    - `.qb-modal` — fullscreen overlay with card
75.    - Mobile breakpoints: single column < 768px, sidebar below features
76. 
77. ---
78. 
79. ### Phase 2 — Enhancements (after MVP validated)
80. 
81. 7. **d1-setup** — Add Cloudflare D1 database for quote history
82.    - Create `wrangler.jsonc` D1 binding
83.    - Create migration: `quotes` table (quote_number, customer_name, customer_email, industry_id, selected_ai_features JSON, selected_modules JSON, total_cost, estimated_days, created_at)
84.    - `POST /api/quotes` Hono endpoint — validate body with Zod, insert to D1, return quote_number
85.    - `GET /api/quotes/:id` — retrieve saved quote
86. 
87. 8. **email-integration** — SendGrid quote email
88.    - `POST /api/quotes/:id/send-email` Hono endpoint
89.    - Uses SendGrid HTTP API (fetch-based, no Node.js SDK needed for CF Workers)
90.    - Attaches PDF as base64 encoded attachment
91.    - `SENDGRID_API_KEY` env var in `wrangler.jsonc` vars
92. 
93. 9. **quote-sharing** — Share link
94.    - After save to D1, show shareable URL: `/quote-builder?quote=SGC-XXXXXX`
95.    - On load, if `?quote=` param present, fetch quote from D1 and pre-populate selections
96. 
97. ---
98. 
99. ## File Map
100. 
101. ```
102. src/
103.   data/
104.     quote-data.ts              ← NEW (Phase 1)
105.   utils/
106.     pricing.ts                 ← NEW (Phase 1)
107.   routes/
108.     quote-builder.tsx          ← NEW (Phase 1)
109.   index.tsx                    ← MODIFIED (Phase 1 — add route + nav link)
110. 
111. public/static/
112.   quote-builder.js             ← NEW (Phase 1)
113.   style.css                    ← MODIFIED (Phase 1 — add .qb-* styles)
114. ```
115. 
116. ---
117. 
118. ## Important Constraints
119. 
120. - **No React, no Tailwind utilities in markup** — follow the existing Hono JSX + `class` pattern
121. - **Dark navy design** — quote builder uses the existing CSS tokens (`--navy-900`, `--cyan`, `--glass-bg`) not the light theme from the copilot.md spec
122. - **jsPDF via CDN** — `https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/jspdf/2.5.1/jspdf.umd.min.js` loaded in the renderer (or page-specific head) with `defer`
123. - **No database for Phase 1** — all state is in-browser; quote number is ephemeral
124. - **PDF coloring** — match brand: navy `#0b0e27` header, cyan `#00d9ff` accents, white text; not the spec's `#3B82F6`/`#F3F4F6` light scheme
125. - **Cloudflare Workers constraints** — no `fs`, no `puppeteer`, PDF must be client-side
126. - **One Hono app** — mount via `app.route()`, do not create a separate Hono instance for the route
127. 
128. ---
129. 
130. ## Notes
131. 
132. - The copilot.md Section 6 Hono route incorrectly uses `import { c } from 'hono/client'` — disregard this; use standard Hono route handler pattern matching the existing `src/index.tsx`
133. - The spec's React component `className` → translate to `class` in Hono JSX
134. - The jsPDF `autoTable` plugin may be needed for the itemized table in the PDF — add it via CDN alongside jsPDF
135. - Lucide icons referenced in data file icon fields are for documentation — in the Hono JSX, use inline SVG matching the existing `Icons.tsx` pattern
136.
````

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 36m 26s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

**No existing test infrastructure.** Here's the full picture of what's pending across code review, QA, and E2E:

---

## 🔍 Code Review — Phase 1 Issues to Address

| # | File | Issue |
|---|------|-------|
| CR-1 | `src/routes/quote-builder.tsx` | The route uses `import { renderer } from '../renderer'` but `renderer` calls `app.use(renderer)` — the sub-app inherits the renderer from the parent already (via `app.route()`). Double-applying it is harmless but worth verifying |
| CR-2 | `src/routes/quote-builder.tsx` | `QbIcon` renders inline SVG via `innerHTML` — this is correct Hono JSX pattern, but the `QbIcons` map has ~30 entries; unused icons in production add ~1KB of dead code |
| CR-3 | `public/static/quote-builder.js` | `window.jspdf` is accessed after `defer` scripts load, but if jsPDF CDN fails (network error), `generatePDF()` shows an `alert()` — should be a non-blocking toast instead |
| CR-4 | `src/renderer.tsx` | jsPDF + autoTable CDN scripts are loaded on **every page** (home, admin) — they should only load on `/quote-builder` |
| CR-5 | `src/data/quote-data.ts` | Needs verification that all 34 entries' `recommendedAiFeatures` / `recommendedModules` reference valid IDs |

---

## 🧪 QA — Manual Test Checklist

**Pricing formula correctness:**
- [ ] Select 1 industry, 0 features → `days = ceil(avgDays × 1)`, support cost = days × 800
- [ ] Select 10 features/modules → complexity multiplier should cap correctly
- [ ] Retainer checkbox adds exactly AED 8,000
- [ ] VAT = `round(subtotal × 0.05)` (not truncated)
- [ ] Total = subtotal + VAT

**PDF generation:**
- [ ] Download triggers with name entered
- [ ] "Name required" error shows if field is blank
- [ ] PDF has correct customer name / company on cover page
- [ ] Line items match sidebar totals exactly
- [ ] Quote number format is `SGC-XXXXXX`

**UX flow:**
- [ ] Industry card shows `aria-pressed="true"` when selected
- [ ] Builder section animates in / scrolls into view after industry select
- [ ] Modal closes on Escape key and overlay click
- [ ] Recommended features are visually highlighted (not auto-checked)

---

## 🎭 E2E / UI Tests — What Should Be Built

No test runner exists. The two best options:

| Option | Effort | Best For |
|--------|--------|---------|
| **Playwright** | Medium — add `@playwright/test` | Full E2E browser automation, PDF download verification, cross-browser |
| **Vitest + happy-dom** | Low — add `vitest` | Unit test `calculateQuote()` pricing logic only |

**Recommended minimum test suite:**

1. **Unit tests** (`vitest`) — `calculateQuote()` pricing formula, edge cases (0 features, max features, retainer on/off)
2. **E2E tests** (`Playwright`) — industry selection → feature toggle → price update → modal → PDF download

---

## 📋 Phase 2 Pending (3 todos)

| Todo | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `d1-setup` | Cloudflare D1 DB — save quotes, `POST /api/quotes`, `GET /api/quotes/:id` |
| `email-integration` | SendGrid — email PDF as attachment via fetch-based HTTP API |
| `quote-sharing` | `/quote-builder?quote=SGC-XXXXXX` — pre-populate from D1 on load |

---

**Highest priority fix right now:** CR-4 (jsPDF loading on all pages). Want me to fix that and then set up the Playwright + Vitest test suites?
